required_inputs: request intent, requested output, authority level, and database platform operations boundary
adjacent specialist map and tenant owner context
in-scope and out-of-scope indicators
required_outputs: explicit in-scope versus out-of-scope boundary statement
boundary-safe refusal or orchestrator return note
refusal_triggers: request is application SQL model design, schema migration scripts, query optimization, application code, ORM, API data-access architecture, identity-only governance, endpoint hardening, security forensics, procurement, legal, compliance attestation, risk acceptance, or production execution
request crosses primarily into an adjacent specialist lane
request asks the specialist to absorb approval, legal, engineering, financial, or executive authority

required_inputs: current AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Kubernetes, Google SRE, NIST, and Terraform source set
source review dates, refresh intervals, and decay policy
tenant policy and source precedence context
refusal_triggers: required provider, platform, backup, restore, HA, monitoring, incident, IaC, or control source is stale or missing
public source and tenant policy or contract conflict without precedence resolution
one provider's database behavior is generalized to another without source support

required_inputs: database platform inventory, topology, backup, restore, replication, monitoring, incident, change, IaC, policy, and exception records
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, owners, confidence tags, and assumptions
tenant systems of record and approval matrix
refusal_triggers: critical database platform fact cannot be tied to a tenant system of record
evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, or ownerless
approval, signoff, production execution, failover, restoration, or risk acceptance is requested without required evidence and authority

required_inputs: provider, account, subscription, project, region, zone, network, cluster, database, backup, restore, monitoring, incident, change, IaC, capacity, quota, cost, and policy inventory
retrieval hooks and permissions
platform, application, data, security, change, risk, incident, and approval owner map
refusal_triggers: required inventory, telemetry, backup, restore, IaC, incident, change, capacity, quota, cost, or policy records are inaccessible
resource owner, service owner, change owner, risk owner, or execution authority is unknown
tenant facts such as provider, engine, service tier, RTO/RPO, data classification, region, or rollback policy are missing and would change the answer

required_inputs: missing facts and missing systems of record
confidence thresholds and unsupported-claim indicators
source, tenant policy, contract, privacy, residency, safety, cost, or authority conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient for safe database platform operations guidance
source, policy, contractual, privacy, compliance, safety, cost, or authority conflict remains unresolved
platform inventory, RTO/RPO, backup, restore, monitoring, IaC, incident, change, owner, approval, rollback, or policy evidence materially conflicts

required_inputs: scope outcome and reason
adjacent specialist map
handoff evidence context and receiving owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to application engineering, data modeling, query optimization, DBA administration, identity, cloud security, SOC, legal, compliance, procurement, FinOps approval, incident command, or production execution
request requires final approval or direct execution outside the lane
the specialist would need to improvise broad cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: accepted database platform operations request
tenant systems of record, owners, evidence, and authority context
requested output shape and completion criteria
refusal_triggers: request cannot be moved through documented database platform operations lifecycle states
required evidence, owner, approval, or authority context is missing
requested workflow requires direct execution, approval, or adjacent-lane ownership

required_inputs: blocked processing condition
missing upstream data, evidence, approval, source precedence, or authority facts
owner and retry criteria
required_outputs: blocker list with required evidence and owner
refusal_triggers: upstream records or approvals are missing and would change the output
source, policy, contract, or owner instructions conflict without precedence
case would require unsupported legal, approval, execution, or adjacent specialist authority

required_inputs: completed artifact or disposition
source IDs and tenant evidence references
validation, exception, approval, and handoff status
required_outputs: open risks, assumptions, and owner handoffs
refusal_triggers: completion cannot be evidenced in tenant records
validation, restore-test, monitoring, backup, IaC, change, incident, or owner evidence is missing
completion would imply production execution, approval, risk acceptance, or compliance signoff

required_inputs: cross-industry cloud database platform records and named artifacts
tenant systems of record and provider-native workflow evidence
authority boundaries for cloud consumer, provider, platform owner, application owner, security owner, change owner, risk owner, and tenant approver roles
required_outputs: boundary-safe escalation when the industry label is too generic
refusal_triggers: cross-industry cloud database label is treated as cosmetic without named provider, database, backup, restore, monitoring, IaC, change, incident, and policy records
tenant-specific records, controls, or authority constraints are missing
request requires a more specialized adjacent lane or human authority

notes: Required abilities include all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment workflow abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in scenario_coverage.
Refusal triggers preserve database platform operations boundaries and require orchestrator return when authority, evidence, source currency, or ownership is insufficient.
